_comment: DRAFT question set for the SearXNG-vs-Parallel search benchmark. gold_answer and gold_urls are intentionally empty/null and MUST be verified against live sources during the build step, not filled from memory. time_sensitive=true questions are judged live at run time (no frozen gold answer). expected_mode drives the mode-matched pairing (quick<->turbo, research/exact<->advanced).

tiers.A: Navigational — find one obvious authoritative page
tiers.D: Niche long-tail — coverage depth beyond popular pages

questions.question: What is the current monthly price in USD of GitHub Copilot Pro for individuals?
questions.gold_answer: GitHub Copilot Pro for individuals is $10 USD per month (or $100/year) (as of 2026-07). It is free for verified students, teachers, and maintainers of popular open-source projects.

questions.question: Which Python version first introduced structural pattern matching (the match/case statement)?
questions.gold_answer: Python 3.10 (released October 2021) introduced structural pattern matching (match/case) via PEP 634 (specification) with PEP 635/636.

questions.question: What is the default TCP port number that PostgreSQL listens on?
questions.gold_answer: 5432. Per the official docs, the 'port' parameter is '5432 by default' (also the IANA-registered port for PostgreSQL).

questions.question: According to the US NIH Office of Dietary Supplements, what is the recommended daily allowance of vitamin C for adult men, in milligrams?
questions.gold_answer: 90 mg/day for adult men (per the NIH Office of Dietary Supplements RDA table). Smokers need an additional 35 mg/day.

questions.question: What is the current Active LTS major version of Node.js?
questions.gold_answer: Node.js 24 (codename Krypton) is the Active LTS line (as of 2026-07). Node.js 22 (Jod) is in Maintenance LTS and Node.js 26 is the Current release (enters LTS in Oct 2026).

questions.question: What is the latest stable minor release of Kubernetes?
questions.gold_answer: Kubernetes 1.36 is the latest stable minor release, with 1.36.2 the latest patch (released 2026-06-09) (as of 2026-07). v1.37 is scheduled for late August 2026.

questions.question: What is the most recent stable release version of the Rust compiler (rustc)?
Which city will host the next Summer Olympic Games, and in what year?
questions.gold_answer: Los Angeles, USA, will host the next Summer Olympic Games, in 2028 (LA28), July 14–30, 2028 (as of 2026-07; the Milan–Cortina Winter Games were held Feb 2026).

questions.question: Compare the free-tier storage (in GB) offered by Google Drive, Dropbox, and Apple iCloud.
questions.gold_answer: Google Drive/Google One: 15 GB free (shared across Gmail, Drive, Photos); Dropbox Basic: 2 GB free; Apple iCloud: 5 GB free. Google Drive offers the most at 15 GB (as of 2026-07).

questions.question: How does the caffeine content of a single standard espresso shot compare to that of one 250ml can of Red Bull?
questions.gold_answer: A single 1 oz (30 ml) espresso shot has roughly 63 mg of caffeine (USDA FoodData Central), while one 250 ml can of Red Bull Energy Drink has 80 mg (Red Bull official). They are roughly comparable, with the Red Bull can holding about 17 mg more caffeine than a single espresso shot.
questions.grading_notes: Espresso caffeine legitimately ranges ~47-75 mg by preparation. Grade on the COMPARISON being correct (roughly comparable, with a 250ml Red Bull can slightly higher at ~80 mg), not on an exact espresso figure. Red Bull 250ml = 80 mg should be present.

questions.question: What is the difference in EPA-estimated maximum driving range between the base Tesla Model 3 and the base Hyundai Ioniq 6?
questions.gold_answer: The base Tesla Model 3 (Standard/RWD) is EPA-rated at 321 miles; the base Hyundai Ioniq 6 (SE Standard Range RWD) is EPA-rated at 240 miles. The Tesla's base range is about 81 miles greater (as of 2026-07; figures are for the base/entry trims of each).
questions.grading_notes: "Base" trim is ambiguous and EPA figures shift by model year/trim. Accept any answer that names specific comparable base/entry trims with correct EPA figures and a correct difference, OR that correctly states the Tesla Model 3 base has meaningfully more range than the Ioniq 6 base (~60-90 mi). Do not penalize a defensible trim choice that differs from the gold figures.

questions.question: Which has the larger metropolitan-area population, Toronto or Chicago, and by roughly how much?
questions.gold_answer: Chicago's metropolitan area is larger. The Chicago–Naperville–Elgin MSA is about 9.4 million (US Census, 2022 est. 9,441,957) versus the Toronto CMA at about 6.2 million (2021 Canadian census, 6,202,225) — Chicago larger by roughly 3.2 million (as of 2026-07). Note the two use different national metro definitions (US MSA vs Canadian CMA).
questions.grading_notes: Grade primarily on the correct conclusion: Chicago's metro is larger, by roughly 3 million (accept ~2.5-3.5M). Exact figures may vary with census year and metro definition (US MSA vs Canadian CMA); do not require exact numbers.

questions.question: What two entropy-coding methods does the Zstandard (zstd) compression format use in its entropy stage?
questions.gold_answer: Huffman coding (used to compress literals) and FSE — Finite State Entropy, an ANS-based (tANS) coder used for the sequence symbols (literals-length, match-length, and offset codes) and to compress the Huffman headers.

questions.question: What is the default value of the work_mem configuration parameter in a stock PostgreSQL installation?
questions.gold_answer: 4MB (four megabytes), per the official PostgreSQL documentation.

questions.question: Who chaired the committee that designed the Voyager Golden Record, and in what year was it launched?
questions.gold_answer: The committee was chaired by Carl Sagan (of Cornell University). The Voyager spacecraft carrying the records were launched in 1977.

questions.question: What is the maximum symmetric data throughput specified by USB4 Version 2.0?
questions.gold_answer: 80 Gbps in symmetric mode (two lanes at 40 Gbps each, bidirectional), using PAM3 signaling. An optional asymmetric mode reaches up to 120 Gbps in one direction (with 40 Gbps in the other). Published by USB-IF on 2022-10-18.

questions.question: Who is Michael I. Jordan the computer scientist (not the basketball player), and what field is he known for?
questions.gold_answer: Michael I. Jordan is an American professor at the University of California, Berkeley (Departments of EECS and Statistics). He is known for machine learning, statistics, and artificial intelligence — one of the most influential figures in machine learning (e.g., graphical models, variational inference, latent Dirichlet allocation).

questions.question: What is the population of Springfield? (Note the ambiguity: multiple US cities share this name.)
questions.gold_answer: Ambiguous — many US cities are named Springfield. A good answer should surface this ambiguity. The most prominent is Springfield, Illinois (the state capital), population 114,394 at the 2020 US Census. Note that the most populous Springfield is actually Springfield, Missouri (~169,000 in 2020), so a strong answer flags the ambiguity and specifies which Springfield.

questions.question: What is Python's Global Interpreter Lock (GIL), and is it being removed or made optional in recent versions?
questions.gold_answer: The GIL is a mutex in CPython that allows only one thread to execute Python bytecode at a time, preventing true multi-core parallelism within a single process. It is being made optional (not removed by default): a free-threaded build (PEP 703) shipped as experimental in Python 3.13 (2024), and Python 3.14 (Oct 2025) made the free-threaded build officially supported but still optional (PEP 779), distributed as a separate '3.14t' binary. Making GIL-free the default and eventual removal are longer-term goals, not yet in effect (as of 2026-07).

questions.question: In the context of machine-learning model evaluation, what does the acronym 'ROC' stand for?
questions.gold_answer: Receiver Operating Characteristic (as in the ROC curve, which plots true positive rate vs. false positive rate).
